steps.title: Use a two-point exponential setup
steps.content: A $3\%$ annual increase means one year after the starting point the population is $\left(6000\right)(1.03)=6180$. Use the values at $x=0$ and $x=1$ directly.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: In an exponential model $p=a(b)^x$, the value at $x=0$ is $a$, so $a=6000$. A $3\%$ annual increase means the growth factor is $b=1+0.03=1.03$. The model is $p=6000(1.03)^x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• "$p=0.97(6,000)^{x}$": $0.97$ is a decay factor and $(6000)^x$ is not the right growth form. Eliminated.
  • "$p=1.03(6,000)^{x}$": uses $6000$ as the base raised to $x$, so the initial value is wrong. Eliminated.
  • "$p=6,000(0.97)^{x}$": uses $0.97$, a $3\%$ decrease. Eliminated.
  • "$p=6,000(1.03)^{x}$": initial population $6000$ multiplied by growth factor $1.03$ each year. Correct.
$p=6,000(1.03)^{x}$ is correct.
